modules.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake
documentation: Fake urllib HTTP connection objects.
Copyright 2008 Google Inc. All Rights Reserved.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.initialize
documentation: Install TestHandler as the only active handler for http requests.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.create_response_key
documentation:
Create the response key for a request.

Args:
    method: The http method (e.g. 'get', 'put', etc.)
    url: The path being requested including site.
    request_headers: Dictionary of headers passed along with the request.
Returns:
    The key as a string.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.dictionary_to_canonical_str
documentation:
Create canonical string from a dictionary.

Args:
    dictionary: The dictionary to convert.
Returns:
    A string of the dictionary in canonical form.

documentation: Base exception type for this module.

classes.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.TestHandler

methods.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.TestHandler.set_response
documentation:
Set a static response to be returned for all requests.

Args:
    response: A FakeResponse object to be returned.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.TestHandler.respond_to
documentation:
Build a response object to be used for a specific request.

Args:
    method: The http method (e.g. 'get', 'put' etc.)
    path: The path being requested (e.g. '/collection/id.json')
    request_headers: Dictionary of headers passed along with the request
    body: The string that should be returned for a matching request
    code: The http response code to return
    response_headers: Dictionary of headers to return
Returns:
    None

methods.name: pyactiveresource.testing.http_fake.TestHandler.do_open
documentation:
Return the response object for the given request.

Overrides the HTTPHandler method of the same name to return a
FakeResponse instead of creating any network connections.

Args:
    http_class: The http protocol being used.
    request: A urllib.request.Request object.
Returns:
    A FakeResponse object.
A urllib handler object which returns a predefined response.

modules.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection

Copyright 2008 Google Inc. All Rights Reserved.

documentation: The base exception class for this module.

documentation: Constructor for FakeConnection object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection._split_path
documentation: Return the path and the query string as a dictionary.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection.respond_to
documentation:
Set the response for a given request.

Args:
    method: The http method (e.g. 'get', 'put' etc.).
    path: The path being requested (e.g. '/collection/id.json')
    headers: Dictionary of headers passed along with the request.
    data: The data being passed in as the request body.
    body: The string that should be returned for a matching request.
    response_headers: The headers returned for a matching request
Returns:
    None

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection.get
documentation: Perform an HTTP get request.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection.post
documentation: Perform an HTTP post request.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection.put
documentation: Perform an HTTP post request.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.fake_connection.FakeConnection.delete
documentation: Perform an HTTP delete request.

A fake HTTP connection for testing.

Inspired by ActiveResource's HttpMock class. This class is designed to
take a list of inputs and their corresponding outputs.

Inputs will be matched on the method, path, query and data arguments

Example:
\>>> connection = FakeConnection()
\>>> body = '\<?json ... />'
\>>> connection.respond_to('get', '/foos/1.json', None, None, body)
\>>> class Foo(resource.Resource):
... \_site = '<http://localhost/>'
...
\>>> Foo.\_connection_obj = connection
\>>> Foo.find(1)
foo(1)

modules.name: pyactiveresource.util
documentation: Utilities for pyActiveResource.
Copyright 2008 Google Inc. All Rights Reserved.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.date_parse
documentation:
Return a datetime object for the given ISO8601 string.

Args:
    time_string: An ISO8601 timestamp.
Returns:
    A datetime.datetime object.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.pluralize
documentation:
Convert singular word to its plural form.

Args:
    singular: A word in its singular form.

Returns:
    The word in its plural form.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.singularize
documentation:
Convert plural word to its singular form.

Args:
    plural: A word in its plural form.
Returns:
    The word in its singular form.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.camelize
documentation:
Convert a word from lower_with_underscores to CamelCase.

Args:
    word: The string to convert.
Returns:
    The modified string.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.underscore
documentation:
Convert a word from CamelCase to lower_with_underscores.

Args:
    word: The string to convert.
Returns:
    The modified string.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.to_query
documentation:
Convert a dictionary to url query parameters.

Args:
    query_params: A dictionary of arguments.
Returns:

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.xml_pretty_format
documentation:
Add PrettyPrint formatting to an ElementTree element.

Args:
    element: An ElementTree element which is modified in-place.
Returns:

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.serialize
documentation:
Write a serialized value to an xml element.

Args:
    value: The value to serialize.
    element: An xml element to write to.
Returns:
    None

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.to_json
documentation:
Convert a dictionary, list or Collection to an JSON string.

Args:
    obj: The object to serialize.

Returns:
    A json string.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.json_to_dict
documentation:
Parse the json into a dictionary of attributes.

Args:
    jsonstr: A JSON formatted string.
Returns:
    The deserialized object.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.to_xml
documentation:
Convert a dictionary or list to an XML string.

Args:
    obj: The dictionary/list object to convert.
    root: The name of the root xml element.
    pretty: Whether to pretty-format the xml (default False).
    header: Whether to include an xml header (default True).
    dasherize: Whether to convert underscores to dashes in
               attribute names (default True).
Returns:
    An xml string.

functions.name: pyactiveresource.util.xml_to_dict
documentation:
Parse the xml into a dictionary of attributes.

Args:
    xmlobj: An ElementTree element or an xml string.
    saveroot: Keep the xml element names (ugly format)
Returns:
    An ElementDict object or ElementList for multiple objects

classes.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Response

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Response.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new Response object.

code, body, headers, msg are retrievable as instance attributes.
Individual headers can be retrieved using dictionary syntax (i.e.
response['header'] => value.

Args:
    code: The HTTP response code returned by the server.
    body: The body of the response.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers.
    msg: The HTTP message (e.g. 200 OK => 'OK').
    response: The original httplib.HTTPResponse (if any).

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Response.from_httpresponse
documentation:
Create a Response object based on an httplib.HTTPResponse object.

Args:
    response: An httplib.HTTPResponse object.
Returns:
    A Response object.
Represents a response from the http server.

classes.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new Connection object.

Args:
    site: The base url for connections (e.g. 'http://foo')
    user: username for basic authentication.
    password: password for basic authentication.
    timeout: socket timeout.
    format: format object for en/decoding resource data.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection._parse_site
documentation:
Retrieve the auth information and base url for a site.

Args:
    site: The URL to parse.
Returns:
    A tuple containing (site, username, password).

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection._request
documentation:
Return a new request object.

Args:
    url: The url to connect to.
Returns:
    A Request object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection._open
documentation:
Perform an HTTP request.

Args:
    method: The HTTP method (GET, PUT, POST, DELETE).
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
    data: The data to send as the body of the request.
Returns:
     A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection._urlopen
documentation:
Wrap calls to urllib so they can be overriden.

Args:
    request: A Request object.
Returns:
    An httplib.HTTPResponse object.
Raises:
    urllib.error.HTTPError on server errors.
    urllib.error.URLError on IO errors.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.get
documentation:
Perform an HTTP get request.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
Returns:
    A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.get_formatted
documentation:
Perform an HTTP get request and return the formatted response.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
Returns:
    The resource as a dict.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.delete
documentation:
Perform an HTTP delete request.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
Returns:
    A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.put
documentation:
Perform an HTTP put request.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
    data: The data to send as the body of the request.
Returns:
    A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.post
documentation:
Perform an HTTP post request.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
    data: The data to send as the body of the request.
Returns:
    A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection.head
documentation:
Perform an HTTP put request.

Args:
    path: The HTTP path to retrieve.
    headers: A dictionary of HTTP headers to add.
Returns:
    A Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.connection.Connection._handle_error
documentation:
Handle an HTTP error.

Args:
    err: A urllib.error.HTTPError object.
Returns:
    An HTTP response object if the error is recoverable.
Raises:
    Redirection: if HTTP error code 301,302 returned.
    BadRequest: if HTTP error code 400 returned.
    UnauthorizedAccess: if HTTP error code 401 returned.
    ForbiddenAccess: if HTTP error code 403 returned.
    ResourceNotFound: if HTTP error code 404 is returned.
    MethodNotAllowed: if HTTP error code 405 is returned.
    ResourceConflict: if HTTP error code 409 is returned.
    ResourceInvalid: if HTTP error code 422 is returned.
    ClientError: if HTTP error code falls in 401 - 499.
    ServerError: if HTTP error code falls in 500 - 599.
    ConnectionError: if unknown HTTP error code returned.
A connection object to interface with REST services.

modules.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource
documentation: Connect to and interact with a REST server and its objects.
Authors: Jared Kuolt <me@superjared.com>, Mark Roach <mrroach@google.com>

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.find
documentation:
Core method for finding resources.

Args:
    id_: A specific resource to retrieve.
    from_: The path that resources will be fetched from.
    kwargs: any keyword arguments for query.

Returns:
    An ActiveResource object.
Raises:
    connection.Error: On any communications errors.
    Error: On any other errors.
Public class methods which act as factory functions

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.find_first
documentation:
Core method for finding resources.

Args:
    from_: The path that resources will be fetched from.
    kwargs: any keyword arguments for query.

Returns:
    The first resource from the list of returned resources or None if
    none are found.
Raises:
    connection.Error: On any communications errors.
    Error: On any other errors.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.find_one
documentation:
Get a single resource from a specific URL.

Args:
    from_: The path that resources will be fetched from.

Returns:
    An ActiveResource object.
Raises:
    connection.Error: On any communications errors.
    Error: On any other errors.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.exists
documentation:
Check whether a resource exists.

Args:

    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for query.
Returns:

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.create
documentation:
Create and save a resource with the given attributes.

Args:
    attributes: A dictionary of attributes which represent this object.
Returns:
    The new resource (which may or may not have been saved successfully).

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._split_options
documentation:
Split prefix options and query options.

Args:
    options: A dictionary of prefix and/or query options.
Returns:
    A tuple containing (prefix_options, query_options)
Non-public class methods to support the above

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._find_single
documentation:
Get a single object from the default URL.

Args:
    id_: The id or other key which specifies a unique object.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    An ActiveResource object.
Raises:
    ConnectionError: On any error condition.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._find_one
documentation:
Find a single resource from a one-off URL.

Args:
    from_: The path from which to retrieve the resource.
    query_options: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    An ActiveResource object.
Raises:
    connection.ConnectionError: On any error condition.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._find_every
documentation:
Get all resources.

Args:
    from_: (optional) The path from which to retrieve the resource.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A list of resources.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._build_object
documentation:
Create an object or objects from the given resource.

Args:
    attributes: A dictionary representing a resource.
    prefix_options: A dict of prefixes to add to the request for
                    nested URLs.
Returns:
    An ActiveResource object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._build_collection
documentation:
Create a Collection of objects from the given resources.

Args:
    elements: A list of dictionaries representing resources.
    prefix_options: A dict of prefixes to add to the request for
                    nested URLs.
    headers: The response headers that came with the resources.
Returns:
    A Collection of ActiveResource objects.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._query_string
documentation:
Return a query string for the given options.

Args:
    query_options: A dictionary of query keys/values.
Returns:
    A string containing the encoded query.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._element_path
documentation:
Get the element path for the given id.

Examples:
    Comment.element_path(1, {'post_id': 5}) -> /posts/5/act
Args:
    id_: The id of the object to retrieve.
    prefix_options: A dict of prefixes to add to the request for
                    nested URLs.
    query_options: A dict of items to add to the query string for
                   the request.
Returns:
    The path (relative to site) to the element formatted with the query.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._collection_path
documentation:
Get the collection path for this object type.

Examples:
    Comment.collection_path() -> /comments.xml
    Comment.collection_path(query_options={'active': 1})
        -> /comments.xml?active=1
    Comment.collection_path({'posts': 5})
        -> /posts/5/comments.xml
Args:
    prefix_options: A dict of prefixes to add to the request for
                    nested URLs
    query_options: A dict of items to add to the query string for
                   the request.
Returns:
    The path (relative to site) to this type of collection.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._custom_method_collection_url
documentation:
Get the collection path for this resource type.

Args:
    method_name: The HTTP method being used.
    options: A dictionary of query/prefix options.
Returns:
    The path (relative to site) to this type of collection.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._class_get
documentation:
Get a nested resource or resources.

Args:

    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._class_head

Predicate a nested resource or resources exists.

Args:

    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._prefix_parameters
documentation:
Return a list of the parameters used in the site prefix.

e.g. /objects/$object_id would yield ['object_id']
     /objects/${object_id}/people/$person_id/ would yield
     ['object_id', 'person_id']
Args:
    None
Returns:
    A set of named parameters.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._prefix
documentation:
Return the prefix for this object type.

Args:

Returns:
    A string containing the path to this element.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.is_valid

   An id string.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.destroy

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.__getattr__
documentation:
Retrieve the requested attribute if it exists.

Args:
    name: The attribute name.
Returns:
    The attribute's value.
Raises:
    AttributeError: if no such attribute exists.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource.__setattr__
documentation:
Set the named attributes.

Args:
    name: The attribute name.
    value: The attribute's value.
Returns:
    None

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._update
documentation:
Update the object with the given attributes.

Args:
    attributes: A dictionary of attributes.
Returns:
    None

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._find_class_for_collection
documentation:
Look in the parent modules for classes matching the element name.

One or both of element/class name must be specified.

Args:
    collection_name: The name of the collection type.
Returns:
    A Resource class.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._find_class_for
documentation:
Look in the parent modules for classes matching the element name.

One or both of element/class name must be specified.

Args:
    element_name: The name of the element type.
    class_name: The class name of the element type.
    create_missing: Whether classes should be auto-created if no
        existing match is found.
Returns:
    A Resource class.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._custom_method_element_url
documentation:
Get the element path for this type of object.

Args:
    method_name: The HTTP method being used.
    options: A dictionary of query/prefix options.
Returns:
    The path (relative to site) to the element formatted with the query.
methods corresponding to Ruby's custom_methods

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._custom_method_new_element_url
documentation:
Get the element path for creating new objects of this type.

Args:
    method_name: The HTTP method being used.
    options: A dictionary of query/prefix options.
Returns:
    The path (relative to site) to the element formatted with the query.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._instance_get
documentation:
Get a nested resource or resources.

Args:
    method_name: the nested resource to retrieve.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A dictionary representing the returned data.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._instance_post
documentation:
Create a new resource/nested resource.

Args:
    method_name: the nested resource to post to.
    body: The data to send as the body of the request.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A connection.Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._instance_put
documentation:
Update a nested resource.

Args:
    method_name: the nested resource to update.
    body: The data to send as the body of the request.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A connection.Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._instance_delete
documentation:
Delete a nested resource or resources.

Args:
    method_name: the nested resource to delete.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A connection.Response object.

methods.name: pyactiveresource.activeresource.ActiveResource._instance_head
documentation:
Predicate a nested resource or resources exists.

Args:
    method_name: the nested resource to predicate exists.
    kwargs: Any keyword arguments for the query.
Returns:
    A connection.Response object.
Represents an activeresource object.
